sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
print("Current nodes:")
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] in ('N', 'W', 'B', 'w', 'c', 'p'):
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} boundary={n['boundary']} children={n['children']}")

ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nRows 25-34, cols 38-54:")
for r in range(25, 35):
    if r < len(ascii_lines):
        print(f"{r:3d}: {ascii_lines[r][38:54]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK, now I see the N+B square is at cols 42-44, and the charcoal door is at cols 44-48. The N+B is partially overlapping with the door. The door's boundary changed to include [[29, 44]] which suggests the N+B is touching/entering it.
Looking at the ascii:
Row 30: `pwwwNNNccccwwwww` - NNN (the N+B square) is at cols 42-44, and cccc (part of door) starts at col 45
Row 31: `pwwwNBNwwwwwwwww` - NBN at cols 42-44
The N+B square is adjacent to the door but not fully inside it. The door is a 5x5 charcoal square with white interior. The N+B is a 3x3 square.
I need to push the N+B square INTO the door. Let me try RIGHT again. But first, let me understand the movement pattern better.
Wait, looking at the agent position: it moved from cols 24-26 to cols 27-29. So the agent IS moving right each time. And the N+B seems to move with it.
Actually, I think the mechanic is: the agent moves, and when it reaches an object, it starts pushing it. The N+B was pushed past the purple bar into the right panel.
Let me try RIGHT again to push N+B further into the door.

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# N+B is at cols 42-44, door is at cols 44-48
# Need to push N+B further right into the door
predict(expect_change=True, note='RIGHT pushes N+B further into the charcoal door')
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
